zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke於大約1235年出生在法國時，他的父親Jean de Lille II31歲，母親Mathilde De Bethune19歲。他於大約1260年與Mahaut de Mortagne在法國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1277年6月之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke于大约1235年出生在法国时，他的父亲Jean de Lille II31岁，母亲Mathilde De Bethune19岁。他于大约1260年与Mahaut de Mortagne在法国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1277年6月之前。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lille
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Lylie, probably from the name of the flower, Middle English lilie, lelie ‘lily’.

English: habitational name from Lelley in East Yorkshire or Lilley in Hertfordshire. The Yorkshire placename probably derives from Old English lǣl ‘twig, brushwood’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. The Hertfordshire placename derives from Old English līn ‘flax’ + lēah.
Irish: Anglicized shortened form of   Allely  .

variants.name: Liley
meanings: English: variant of   Lilly  .

variants.name: Lillie
meanings: English: variant of   Lilly  .

variants.name: Lilley
meanings: English: variant of   Lilly  .

variants.name: Lillis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Ethna, Michael Patrick.
Irish and English: metronymic from   Lilly  .

Greek: from the male or female personal name Lili, a pet form of Vangelis (see Vangel ) or of the related female name Evangelia, or of any of various other male personal names ending in -lis, e.g. Anatolis, Vassilis, Gavrilis.

variants.name: Allely
meanings: Irish: shortened form of Mac Ailghile, which Woulfe described as a ‘branch of the Maguires’ in Fermanagh, also found in the shortened Anglicized forms Callaly and Lilly .

variants.name: Lily
meanings: English: variant of   Lilly  .

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: Jeane
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jean .

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke was born about 1235, in France, his father, Jean de Lille II, was 31 and his mother, Mathilde De Bethune, was 19. He married Mahaut de Mortagne about 1260, in France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died before June 1277.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke est né environ 1235, à France, son père, Jean de Lille II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Mathilde De Bethune, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Mahaut de Mortagne environ 1260, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant juin 1277.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke um 1235 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean de Lille II 31 und seine Mutter Mathilde De Bethune 19. Er hat um 1260 in Frankreich Mahaut de Mortagne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor Juni 1277.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke è nato il circa 1235 nel Francia, il suo padre, Jean de Lille II, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Mathilde De Bethune, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Mahaut de Mortagne circa 1260, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì prima del giugno 1277.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebekeが約1235年にフランスで生まれた時，父Jean de Lille IIは31歳，母Mathilde De Bethuneは19歳であった。約1260年にフランスでMahaut de Mortagneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1277年6月以前に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke(이)가 대략 1235년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean de Lille II(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilde De Bethune(은)는 19세였다. 그는 대략 1260년 프랑스에서 Mahaut de Mortagne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1277년 6월 이전 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke nasceu aproximadamente 1235, em França, seu pai, Jean de Lille II, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Mathilde De Bethune, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Mahaut de Mortagne aproximadamente 1260, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes junho de 1277.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke родился около 1235 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Jean de Lille II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Mathilde De Bethune, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Mahaut de Mortagne около 1260 г. в местности Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер перед июнь 1277 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Jean III de Lille , Chatelain de Lille, Heer zu Meulebeke nació aproximadamente 1235, en Francia, su papá, Jean de Lille II, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Mathilde De Bethune, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Mahaut de Mortagne aproximadamente 1260, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió antes de junio de 1277.
